methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
JetZero is a Series B-stage transportation company founded by Tom O'Leary and Mark Page that has raised over $1.0B to date. The transportation sector comp universe includes seven private peers (Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Arc Boats, The ePlane Company, and two others) and no public comparables, though same-stage and adjacent-stage peers within transportation proved insufficient in number to support a reliable peer-median valuation approach.

The stage-progression method was the applicable triangulation approach here. Using total capital raised of $1.0B as a proxy for implied post-money valuation at the prior stage, a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B-to-subsequent-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $1.9B. This reflects typical venture capital markup patterns as the company advances its development trajectory and de-risks execution risk.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.6B to $2.2B, centered on $1.9B. This estimate carries material uncertainty given the absence of granular peer comps at matching stage and valuation tier. The range incorporates 15% downside and upside variance around the median to reflect both the limited comparative data and the early-stage risk profile inherent to Series B frontier-tech transportation ventures. Further refinement would benefit from closer peer milestones on capital efficiency and technical validation metrics.
